items.title: Thai baht/US dollar weaker on Tuesday
items.summary:
BANGKOK: The Thai baht weakened against the US dollar on Tuesday.
At 0155 GMT, the baht was 0.1% lower at 33.66 versus the dollar, after trading in a range of 33.645 to 33.705.
It ended the previous session at 33.62 per dollar, as per LSEG data.

items.title: Stocks surge at PSX, KSE-100 gains 1,200 points on Middle East mediation news
items.summary:
Strong buying momentum was observed at the Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX) amid reports of mediation efforts between the US and Iran, with the benchmark KSE-100 Index gaining nearly 1,200 points during the trading session on Tuesday.
At 1:30pm, the benchmark index was hovering at 177,121.97, up by 1,194.24 points or 0.68%.
Buying was observed in key sectors, including automobile assemblers, cement,

items.title: Nine dead, 15 trapped after India tunnel collapse
items.summary:
NEW DELHI: At least nine people have died and 15 others remained trapped after a tunnel collapsed at an under-construction hydroelectric project in India’s remote northeast, officials said Tuesday.
The incident occurred Monday in the Himalayan state of Sikkim, where workers were constructing a tunnel for a state-run hydropower project on the Teesta river.
“We are estimating around 25-27 people wer

items.title: SECP clears first IPO of FY27
items.summary:
The Securities and Exchange Commission of Pakistan (SECP) approved on Tuesday the issuance, circulation, and publication of the prospectus for the Initial Public Offering (IPO) of Tasdeeq Information Services Limited (TISL).
“This marks the first IPO approved in FY2026–27, reflecting the continued momentum in Pakistan’s primary capital market,” said the SECP in a press statement.
Tasdeeq Informati

items.title: PSX gains 1,200 points during intraday trade as oil eases amid US-Iran mediation efforts
items.summary:
The Pakistan Stock Exchange’s (PSX) benchmark index gained nearly 1,200 points during intraday trade on Tuesday.
The KSE-100 index opened in the green, soaring by 2,442.72 points to reach an intraday high of 178,370.45 at 10:09am.
However, it then dipped sharply after 12pm, declining to 177,120.04 points at 12:44pm, up by 1,192.31 (0.68 per cent) from the previous close.
The development came as gl

items.title: Kuwait’s KUFPEC eyes expansion in Pakistan’s energy sector
items.summary:
Kuwait Foreign Petroleum Exploration Company (KUFPEC), a subsidiary of the Kuwait Petroleum Corporation (KPC), is looking to expand its presence in Pakistan’s upstream energy sector.
The development came during a meeting between Federal Minister for Petroleum Ali Pervaiz Malik and Tareq Ebrahim, Vice President Operations of KUFPEC, to discuss opportunities for expanding cooperation in Pakistan’s p

items.title: Messi says Argentina's defeat by Spain left immense pain
items.summary:
Lionel Messi said Argentina’s World Cup final defeat by Spain had left “immense” pain but praised his team for reaching ​a second successive final and thanked supporters for uniting behind ‌the national side.
Spain beat Argentina 1-0 after extra time in Sunday’s final in East Rutherford, New Jersey, with Ferran Torres scoring the decisive goal ​to deny the 2022 champions back-to-back World Cup tit

items.title: PM Shehbaz reaffirms support for Gilgit-Baltistan in meeting with CM Amjad
items.summary:
Prime Minister Muhammad Shehbaz Sharif met with the newly appointed chief minister of Gilgit-Baltistan, Amjad Hussain, in Islamabad on Tuesday to discuss overall regional affairs and ongoing development initiatives.
During the meeting, the prime minister congratulated CM Amjad Hussain on assuming office and reiterated the federal government’s firm commitment to working closely with the newly elect

items.title: 'Great American Sleepover' softens US image abroad, but World Cup charm campaign hit some bumps
items.summary:
NEW YORK: A supersized World Cup tournament and its dizzying array of feel-good social media fodder gave the US image a much-needed boost globally, though concerns over high-level interference and visa difficulties for some fans overshadowed the action on the field at ​times as the lines between politics and sport blurred.
An annual study of democracy in May showed that global perceptions of ‌the

items.title: Israeli airstrike kills family of six in Gaza, medics say
items.summary:
CAIRO: An Israeli airstrike on Tuesday killed a father, his wife and their four children in Gaza City, while ​setting their home ablaze, health officials said.
Israel launches deadly strikes on Lebanon’s Tyre after warning
Medics said the ‌Israeli strike on the family house in the Sabra neighbourhood killed the man, Firas Al-Masri, his wife, Salsabeel, and their three daughters and ​son.
The Israe

items.title: New Zealand dollar firms with inflation, Aussie follows
items.summary:
SYDNEY: The New Zealand dollar pushed higher on Tuesday after a surprisingly hot inflation reading cemented expectations for further interest rate hikes, adding to inflationary pressure on bonds from surging fuel prices.
Yield spreads on both New Zealand and Australian bonds widened after Brent touched $90 and the Iran-aligned Houthis threatened a blockade on Saudi Arabian oil shipments.
The shift

items.title: Miners weigh on Australian shares; intensifying US-Iran tensions keep risk sentiment at bay
items.summary:
Australian shares edged lower on Tuesday as weakness in commodity prices dragged miners, while risk appetite was subdued amid escalating tensions between the US and Iran.
The S&P/ASX 200 index lost 0.6%, down at 8,742.3 by 0008 GMT to mark a fourth consecutive session in the red.
The benchmark closed 0.1% lower in the previous session.
In a potential expansion to the Middle East conflict and a

items.title: Three Chinese injured in drone attack on Moscow
items.summary:
BEIJING: Three Chinese nationals were injured during a Ukraine drone attack on Moscow, Chinese state media said on Tuesday.
The Chinese embassy in Russia said two people were receiving treatment at a local hospital and were in stable condition without life-threatening injuries, while another had asked to be discharged for treatment back in China.
The embassy immediately activated its emergency res

items.title: Australian Open to limit cheap tickets after crowding problems
items.summary:
MELBOURNE: The Australian Open will place daily limits on cheap tickets to the tennis Grand Slam for the first time after long queues and crowding blighted the tournament in January.
Organisers Tennis Australia said on Tuesday it would cap allocations of its A$49 ($34) “ground passes” during the first week of the main draw as they expected demand to be stronger than ever in 2027.
“This decision re

items.title: South Korean shares rally after two-day rout, won eases from 10-week high
items.summary:
Round-up of South Korean financial markets:  
South Korean shares rebounded on Tuesday after a two-day rout, helped by gains in chip stocks, while the won eased from a 10-week high against the dollar.
The benchmark KOSPI was up 158.82 points, or 2.44%, at 6,675.09 as of 0129 GMT after losing 10.5% in the prior two sessions.
It has shed over a quarter of its value since its record close on June 22.

items.title: Copper gains on Chinese demand, tight stocks
items.summary:
SINGAPORE: Copper ticked up on Tuesday, supported by fears of supply-side tightness and strong Chinese buying, despite macroeconomic demand worries from the escalation in the US- Iran war.
The benchmark three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange increased 0.29% to $13,661 a metric ton by 0300 GMT.
The most-traded copper contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange gained 1.05% at 104,850 yuan ($

items.title: Corn, soybeans decline as US crop ratings top expectations
items.summary:
SINGAPORE: Chicago corn and soybeans slid on Tuesday as better-than-expected US crop ratings weighed on prices, which climbed to multi-month highs in the previous session on strong demand and renewed fighting in the Middle East.
Wheat prices inched up on worries about Black Sea shipments as escalating attacks on grain cargoes provided support.
“Crop ratings have come in better than what the market

items.title: Julius Baer posts H1 net profit of $831 million
items.summary: ZURICH: Swiss bank Julius Baer on Tuesday reported a net profit of 673 million Swiss francs ($831 million) for the first half of 2026, up 128% from a year earlier, when profits were hit by loan loss provisions.

items.title: Novartis Q2 profit up as generic competition mounts
items.summary:
Novartis’ second-quarter core operating profit beat market expectations on Tuesday, helped by strong sales of cancer and multiple sclerosis drugs, though generic competition for its top-selling heart drug Entresto is mounting.
The Swiss drugmaker saw quarterly group operating income, adjusted for special items, come in at $5.94 billion dollars, above average analyst expectations of about $5.31 bil

items.title: ASEAN ministers voice serious concern over Middle East situation
items.summary:
MANILA: ASEAN foreign ministers expressed serious concern on Tuesday about the rapidly evolving conflict in the Middle East, calling for restraint and the avoidance of acts that further aggravate the situation.
ASEAN envoy meets Myanmar opposition groups in Thailand
The developments severely undermine ongoing efforts by key mediators and diminish prospects for a peaceful resolution through dialogu

items.title: China stocks rise as chip shares rebound; focus turns to Politburo meeting
items.summary:
SHANGHAI: Chinese technology shares staged a roller-coaster ride on Tuesday morning, helping lift key benchmark indexes as semiconductor stocks rebounded sharply from earlier losses.  
The benchmark Shanghai Composite index was up 0.6% by the midday break, while the blue-chip CSI 300 index rose 1.8%.
The tech-focused STAR50 index bounced 7% after falling more than 3%, and the start-up board CHINEX

items.title: Palm falls tracking weaker Dalian rivals, crude oil
items.summary:
JAKARTA: Malaysian palm oil futures edged lower on Tuesday, tracking weakness in rival Dalian vegetable oils and weighed down by softer crude oil prices.
The benchmark palm oil contract for October delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange dropped 40 ringgit, or 0.86%, to 4,603 ringgit ($1,126.81) a metric ton by the midday break.
“Today’s palm market opened lower, following the weakness

items.title: Yuan rises after firmer guidance as investors await policy cues
items.summary:
SHANGHAI: China’s yuan rose against the dollar on Tuesday after the central bank set firmer official guidance, while investors turned their attention to a key policy meeting for clues on the currency’s outlook.
The US dollar hovered near a one-week high as markets weighed mixed signals from the Middle East, where escalating hostilities revived concerns over energy supplies and prospects for a ceas

items.title: Foreign deposit flows to buoy India bonds; war worry may overshadow
items.summary:
MUMBAI: Indian government bonds are poised to open firmer on Tuesday, buoyed by data showing higher-than-expected inflows from the central bank’s overseas deposit push, though caution over the US-Iran war may cap gains.
The benchmark 6.94% 2036 bond yield is seen trading in a 6.76%-6.79% range, a trader at a private sector bank said, after ending Monday at 6.7770%.
Central bank data released on Mo

items.title: Hormuz vessel crossings extend slide on fresh US-Iran attacks
items.summary:
SINGAPORE: Vessel crossings via the Strait of Hormuz dropped further at the start of the week, shipping data showed, as caution grew following a fresh exchange of attacks between the United States and Iran.
A total of four commodity vessels crossed the strait on Monday, mostly on the Iranian route, down from seven the previous day, Kpler data showed.
The four vessels included two tankers that exit

items.title: US technology firm proposes refinery modernisation plan for Pakistan in meeting with finance minister
items.summary:
WASHINGTON: Finance Minister Senator Muhammad Aurangzeb on Monday met a delegation from Honeywell Technologies and discussed a proposed plan to modernise and expand Pakistan’s refinery sector.
According to the finance ministry, Aurangzeb met with a delegation from the company comprising Vice President General Manager Barry Glickman.
“During the meeting, the finance minister welcomed Honeywell’s pr

items.title: Australia welcome Cummins, Hazlewood, Lyon back for Bangladesh Tests
items.summary:
MELBOURNE: Australia captain Pat Cummins, fellow fast bowler Josh Hazlewood and veteran spinner Nathan Lyon have returned from injury to be included in a 13-man squad for next month’s two-Test home series against Bangladesh.
The trio were named on Tuesday for matches in Darwin (Aug. 13-17) and Mackay (Aug. 22-26), which form part of the World Test Championship cycle, after playing only three Tests

items.title: Gold rises as hopes for US-Iran diplomacy pause oil rally
items.summary:
Gold prices rose on Tuesday as investors weighed diplomatic efforts to ease the US-Iran conflict, which could temper oil-driven inflation risks and influence the US Federal Reserve’s interest rate path.
Spot gold rose 0.9% to $4,042.69 per ounce as of 0345 GMT.
US gold futures for August delivery were up 0.8% at $4,047.40.
“It looks like gold is trying to find a base somewhere around this ($4,000)

items.title: US Honeywell proposes refinery upgrade plan for Pakistan
items.summary:
Pakistan is considering a proposal from US conglomerate Honeywell Technologies to modernise and expand the country’s refinery sector to enhance domestic refining capacity and reduce reliance on imported petroleum products.
The development came during a high-level interaction between Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb and a delegation of Honeywell Technologies led by its Vice President and General

items.title: India youth group says will not march again because police will 'hurt youth'
items.summary:
NEW DELHI: India’s youth-led “cockroach” movement said on Tuesday it would continue ​its protest but will not march again, a day after ‌scores of supporters were injured in clashes with police who tried to stop their march on parliament.
Delhi Police said nearly 180 people, including 118 security and police personnel, ​and 60 protesters, were injured on Monday when they clashed in ​central Delhi.

items.title: Pakistan invites Chinese investment in ports & logistics
items.summary:
Pakistan has invited Chinese investors to expand their investments in Pakistan’s ports, logistics, and maritime sectors.
Addressing the Regional Transit Trade Hub Initiative in Beijing, organised by Jiang Tonland International Holdings, Federal Minister for Maritime Affairs Muhammad Junaid Anwar Chaudhry said the government was committed to transforming the country into a regional transit trade, l

items.title: US imposes new 50pc tariffs on $20bn worth of Canadian products
items.summary:
US President Donald Trump unveiled 50 per cent tariffs on a wide range of imports from Canada on Monday in response to what the US administration called its discriminatory treatment of American-made cars, alcohol and dairy goods, threatening a new front in a global trade war.
In slapping import taxes on goods ranging from wine to cement and ice hockey gear, Trump invoked Section 338 of the Tariff

items.title: Govt borrows Rs5.9tr from banks in FY26
Trump seeks damages from Canada over wildfires
items.summary:
JOINT ANDREWS: President Donald Trump said he held talks on Sunday at the World Cup final with Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney and called for damages over wildfires that have sent smoke across the northeast US.
Trump threatened Canada with tariffs before Sunday’s match, which the smoke had threatened to disrupt.
“I have a good relationship with Mark Carney, but you know, we got to stop the fir

items.title: New Zealand and Philippines form comprehensive partnership
items.summary:
SYDNEY: New Zealand and the ​Philippines have formed ‌a comprehensive partnership, New Zealand foreign ​minister Winston Peters ​said on Tuesday.
“New Zealand ⁠and the ​Philippines are close ​friends and trusted partners on the world stage - ​and today ​we turn this friendship into ‌concrete ⁠action,” Peters said.

items.title: FBR cannot hide behind software limitations: federal tax ombudsman
items.summary:
KARACHI: The Federal Tax Ombudsman (FTO) has held that the Federal Board of Revenue (FBR) cannot hide behind the ‘technical limitations’ of its own IRIS software to deprive a taxpayer of a substantive legal right and has directed the board to remove system glitches that were blocking a lawful tax credit under Section 63 of the Income Tax Ordinance 2001.

The ruling came in a complaint filed by a s

items.title: Senate panel reviews tax exemptions, border security
items.summary:
ISLAMABAD: A subcommittee of the Senate Standing Committee on Interior and Narcotics Control on Monday reviewed border security mechanisms at key import entry points, alleged tax evasion through consumption of imported raw material meant for tax-exempt areas, and the affairs of the Senate Employees Housing Cooperative Society.
The meeting was held at the Parliament House under the convenorship of

items.title: Pakistan State Oil to be sole diesel importer in FY27
items.summary:
ISLAMABAD: The government has imposed a complete ban on importing high-speed diesel (HSD) by private oil marketing companies (OMCs). Instead, only the state-owned Pakistan State Oil (PSO) will handle imports for the current fiscal year.

This is part of the federal cabinet’s decisions, which also capped petrol import quantities by private OMCs based on their past sales. The decisions are premised

items.title: Non-textile exports fall in FY26
items.summary:
ISLAMABAD: Exports of non-textile goods plunged 13.77 per cent to $12.21 billion in FY26, down from $14.16bn in the preceding year, as shipments of agricultural produce and value-added products suffered steep declines.
The downturn underscores mounting pressure on key sectors, particularly agriculture, where lower volumes and weakening external demand have eroded earnings. By contrast, only margin

items.title: Stocks eke out gains amid extreme volatility
items.summary:
KARACHI: Amid persistent geopolitical tensions, the Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX) opened the week on a depressed note, with the market remaining under extreme volatility. However, late value-hunting allowed the benchmark KSE-100 index to close the session in the green with a meagre gain.
